pi_items.source: Task description and verifier.py line 28-29 confirm exact URL. Verified as valid HTTP endpoint format for localhost testing with basic auth.
Microsoft Learn Service Hook Events documentation confirms this is a valid Azure DevOps service hook event with publisherId='tfs' and scope='all'.
Task description confirms this username is used for basic authentication. Valid username format for Azure DevOps service hooks. Verifier.py line 99 checks for this exact value.
setup_azure_devops.ps1 (line 138) creates the project with this name during environment initialization. TailwindTraders is a real Microsoft demo project for realistic reference.
setup_azure_devops.ps1 (line 312) creates the default Git repository with this name within the TailwindTraders project during environment initialization.

privileged_info_summary: The task configures a Service Hook in Azure DevOps Server to trigger on pull request creation events. The event type git.pullrequest.created is a verified standard Azure DevOps service hook event published by the TFS publisher. The webhook targets http://localhost:9090/api/compliance-scan using basic authentication with username 'compliance_bot'. The TailwindTraders project and repository are created synthetically during environment setup. All metadata claims are consistent with Azure DevOps architecture and the task description.
